description_long: This is a large, stately brick house with a symmetrical facade and a prominent, dark gabled roof featuring multiple dormer windows and skylights. The front elevation showcases a central entryway with a small porch supported by columns, flanked by evenly spaced, multi-pane windows. On one side, there is an attached garage with two large doors, while the rear of the house features a covered patio or veranda with a flat roof and glass panels. The house has two full stories, with a third partial level visible in the form of attic dormers. The exterior is primarily red brick, complemented by white window frames and trim, and the roof is finished in dark gray or black shingles. The overall design is reminiscent of Colonial Revival architecture, with its balanced proportions and classical details.
description: A large, symmetrical red brick house with a dark gabled roof, dormer windows, and an attached garage. Features include a columned entry porch, multi-pane windows, and a covered rear patio.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch with columns, symmetrical window arrangement, and attached garage on the right.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the garage extension, side windows, and the covered patio area with glass panels.
description_view_2: Back view displaying the full width of the covered patio/veranda, rear windows, and the house's overall rectangular footprint.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the brick chimney, side windows, and the extension of the covered patio structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with small porch, columns, steps, and a decorative overhang
